title: Review the risks of changing Kernel design to be /vote-first in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public. Identify which executable checks, docs, or launcher behaviors would break or become misleading if /kernel remains co-equal. Return prioritized findings and recommended test set. Do not edit...
summary_text: **Prioritized Findings** 1. **Critical: Canary currently allows single-lane fallback (workflow violation).** Evidence: lane checks only fail on `0`/empty, so `1` lane still passes in regular/alternate/rollback paths: [run-canary.sh:996](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:996), [run-canary.sh:1052](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:1052), [run-canary.sh:1114](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh:1114). **Fix:** enforce minimum lanes (for example `>=6`) and fail with `lanes-below-minimum`. **Test:** add a canary test that feeds `lanes_configured=1` and expects failure reason `lanes-below-minimum`. 2. **High: Backup terminal dedupe can suppress legitimate second completion records.** Evidence: for terminal sources, dedupe ignores summary/title differences and dedupes by session+source+time window: [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:134](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:134), [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:148](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:148), [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:371](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:371). Current test explicitly expects this suppression even with changed summary: [test-kernel-task-completion-backup-fallback.sh:36](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup-fallback.sh:36). **Fix:** include summary/title (and ideally Project OS fields) in terminal dedupe key, or add `--force-record` bypass for legitimate rewrites. **Test:** same session/source within 5 min but changed summary should record a second entry; exact duplicate should still dedupe. 3. **High: Timestamp skew/backfill can be wrongly deduped.** Evidence: dedupe condition is `current_ts - latest < cooldown` with no lower bound: [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:158](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:158). If `current_ts < latest` (clock skew/backfill), it is always deduped. **Fix:** require non-negative delta: `0 <= (current_ts - latest) < cooldown`. **Test:** first record at newer timestamp, second at older timestamp should **not** dedupe. 4. **Medium: Missing run context collapses into `unknown-run`, increasing accidental suppression scope.** Evidence: both wrappers default session id to fixed `unknown-run` and pass it to backup dedupe path: [kernel-run-complete.sh:20](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-run-complete.sh:20), [kernel-run-complete.sh:119](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-run-complete.sh:119), [kernel-milestone-record.sh:32](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-milestone-record.sh:32), [kernel-milestone-record.sh:192](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-milestone-record.sh:192), [kernel-milestone-record.sh:261](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-milestone-record.sh:261). **Fix:** fail fast if `KERNEL_RUN_ID` is missing (except explicit dry-run), or generate a unique fallback run id instead of constant `unknown-run`. **Test:** verify command errors (or unique IDs differ per invocation) when `KERNEL_RUN_ID` is unset. **What I ran** - `bash tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup-fallback.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-run-complete.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-milestone-record.sh` - `bash tests/test-codex-kernel-guard-doctor.sh` - `bash tests/test-codex-kernel-guard-doctor-flags.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-canary-plan.sh` All passed; risks above are residual correctness/coverage gaps rather than current red tests.
